title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics are reportedly considering trade offers for Jaylen Brown, seeking at least four first-round picks.
Portland Trail Blazers and Detroit Pistons are potential suitors, with trade packages involving draft picks and players.
Minnesota Timberwolves had interest in Brown but deemed the Celtics' trade demands excessive for their roster fit.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' and 'Superman' are among new films available on Disney+ and HBO Max.
'Avatar: The Last Airbender' Season 2 and 'The Bear' Season 5 now streaming on Netflix and Hulu.
'Life, Larry, and the Pursuit of Unhappiness' featuring Larry David is streaming on HBO Max.

title: Red Sox place Marcelo Mayer on 10-day IL with forearm issue
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Marcelo Mayer placed on 10-day IL with forearm injury, impacting Red Sox lineup.
Tsung-Che Cheng called up from Worcester to play shortstop against the Yankees.
Red Sox face injury challenges with players like Garrett Crochet, Trevor Story, and Roman Anthony also sidelined.

title: Bruins reportedly trade for Utah forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins are reportedly finalizing a trade to acquire Utah forward JJ Peterka, 24, for two first-round picks.
Peterka, a German winger, has a contract through 2029-30 with a $7.7 million cap hit and notable scoring ability.
Bruins' trade continues trend of signing German players under coach Marco Sturm, replacing expected free agent Viktor Arvidsson.

title: Craig Breslow on his job security, whether the Red Sox will buy or sell, and more
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox chief baseball officer Craig Breslow won't concede the season despite being last in the American League East.
Breslow faces uncertainty about job security but remains focused on improving the team's performance and development pipeline.
The team considers selling top players like Sonny Gray as the August trade deadline approaches, evaluating their position in the league.

title: Review: ‘Supergirl’ is a hero in search of a better movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Milly Alcock stars as Supergirl, delivering a strong performance in a film that lacks originality and excitement.
The movie's plot and visuals are criticized for their unoriginality, with tone-setting scenes feeling uninspired.
Flashbacks exploring Supergirl's upbringing provide depth but could have been introduced earlier for more impact.

title: One of Cambridge’s most beloved Japanese restaurants is closing after 34 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Tampopo, a Japanese restaurant in Cambridge, is closing after 34 years as owner Yasumasa Ito retires.
Former employee Kotaro Morita returned from Japan to assist during Tampopo's final weeks of operation.
Longtime customers share memories on social media, highlighting Tampopo's impact on the Cambridge community.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Wide receivers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown joins the Patriots, providing quarterback Drake Maye with a top receiver after Stefon Diggs' departure.
Patriots' wide receivers ranked second in the league last season for big plays and yards per reception.
New additions Brown and Romeo Doubs could require time to integrate into the Patriots' offensive system.

title: Here’s the Celtics’ reported asking price for Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics are reportedly asking for at least four first-round picks for Jaylen Brown in trade talks.
Boston's offer for Giannis Antetokounmpo was rejected as the team continues negotiations involving Jaylen Brown.
Jaylen Brown remains under contract with the Celtics for three more years amid ongoing trade discussions.

title: 4 takeaways from France’s 4-1 win over Norway at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird and Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match at Gillette Stadium, securing first place in Group I.
Ousmane Dembélé scored a first-half hat-trick, making it the second-fastest in World Cup history despite Haaland's absence.
Norway's Jorgen Strand Larsen missed a penalty, with goalkeeper Mike Maignan making a crucial save for France.
